name: chrome
description: Control a Chrome browser via the chrome-devtools-mcp server. Supports navigation, screenshots, DOM inspection, JavaScript evaluation, network monitoring, performance analysis, and more. Each agent gets its own persistent browser profile. The browser process is started on first use and closed automatically after an idle timeout. File downloads are saved to /workspace/media/inbound/.
commands: take_snapshot                                            — take an accessibility tree snapshot of the current page (use this first)
click --uid <uid>                                       — click an element (uid from take_snapshot)
fill_form --elements <json>                             — fill multiple form fields at once
evaluate_script --function <js>                        — evaluate JavaScript in the page
list_network_requests                                   — list network requests since last navigation

slim.description: Launch in slim mode — only navigate, evaluate, screenshot tools are available. Much lower token usage. Default: false.

headless.description: Launch Chrome headlessly (no visible window). Default: false.

channel.description: Chrome channel to use: stable | beta | dev | canary. Default: stable.

viewport.description: Browser viewport size as WxH, e.g. '1280x720'. Default: unset (Chrome default).

idleTimeoutMinutes.description: Automatically close the browser process after this many minutes of inactivity. Default: 30.

version.description: chrome-devtools-mcp npm version to use via npx. Default: latest.

allowTools.description: If set, only these MCP tool names are callable. Omit to allow all.

denyTools.description: These MCP tool names are always blocked, even if in allowTools. Deny beats allow.

noUsageStatistics.description: Opt out of Google usage statistics collection. Default: true.

timeout.description: Timeout per MCP tool call in seconds. Default: 60.

acceptInsecureCerts.description: Accept insecure TLS certificates. Default: false.

executablePath.description: Absolute path to the Chrome or Chromium binary. Overrides auto-detection entirely. Optional.

fallbackToChromium.description: If no Chrome binary is found during auto-detection, try Chromium paths as a fallback. Default: true.

display.description: X11 display for the browser window (Linux only). Use with TigerVNC or other virtual X11 servers, e.g. ':1' opens the browser on virtual screen 1. Defaults to inheriting the gateway's DISPLAY environment variable. Has no effect when headless is true or on macOS/Windows.
